portal_system: CAL-ACCESS (legacy public portal) + Cal-Online (filer side); CARS replacement in active development, public go-live targeted Nov 26 2026
regulator: California Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC, enforcement) + California Secretary of State, Political Reform Division (filings/registry)

seed_urls.notes: Top-level Secretary of State Campaign & Lobbying landing page; hub for CAL-ACCESS, Cal-Online, Power Search, CARS updates
CAL-ACCESS public lobbying search hub; links to Lobbyists, Firms, Employers, $5K+ payments, daily filings, advanced search; the legacy classic-ASP system due for CARS replacement
Alphabetical lobbyist directory, filterable by 2025-2026 session; returns name, filer ID, employer, photo
Lobbyist employers directory; alphabetical and by category; includes payment totals
Registration filing requirements (Forms 601-607); 2-year cycle, $100 first-year fee per lobbyist, 10-day registration window, 20-day amendment window
Bulk-data index page; documents the daily-refreshed tab-delimited extracts and points to the dbwebexport.zip and calaccess-documentation.zip artifacts

Daily-refreshed full CAL-ACCESS database export (tab-delimited tables, ~650MB unzipped, ~80 tables across campaigns + lobbying); WebFetch hit 10MB cap which confirms file is large and live. Stage 2 must download with proper UA + streaming.
CAL-ACCESS schema/format documentation ZIP (~4.1MB); CalFormat spec + errata PDFs describing the bulk-export tables
CAL-ACCESS User's Manual; Section 2 is the Lobbying Finance FAQ; also links to glossary, statutes & regulations, table of forms
FPPC Lobbying Manual + forms hub; 7-chapter manual covering qualification, registration, expense reporting, recordkeeping
FPPC plain-language guide to registration and quarterly reporting obligations; complements the SOS filing pages

Cal. Gov. Code Title 9, Chapter 6 (Lobbyists), sections 86100-86300; Article 1 Registration & Reporting, Article 2 Prohibitions, Article 3 Exemptions. Implements Political Reform Act of 1974 (Prop 9).
FPPC About page; 5-member independent commission, est. 1974 by Prop 9; enforces Political Reform Act
CARS project status page; vendor CGI; public go-live targeted Nov 26 2026 ahead of general election. Worth re-checking quarterly to detect cutover.

flagged.notes: Direct deep-link to Lobbying Finance FAQ (Section 2) returned 404; the parent users-manual page (verified above) is the working entry point and should be used to navigate into Section 2. May indicate a recent SOS site reorg.

Third-party California Civic Data Coalition mirror that re-publishes CAL-ACCESS bulk data as CSV instead of the official tab-delimited format. Useful as a backup/secondary source; should be verified separately before relying on it. Not part of the official portal.

observations: California still runs the legacy CAL-ACCESS public portal (classic-ASP under cal-access.sos.ca.gov) plus the Cal-Online filer-side system. The CARS replacement (vendor: CGI) is in active development with a public go-live target of Nov 26 2026, so this URL set is expected to be in flux and should be re-snapped quarterly. Bulk data is excellent: a single daily-refreshed dbwebexport.zip (~650MB, ~80 tables) covers both campaigns and lobbying, with a separate documentation ZIP describing the CalFormat schema; both live on campaignfinance.cdn.sos.ca.gov and are friendly to programmatic snapshotting (no auth/WAF observed via WebFetch, but Stage 2 should still set a real UA and stream the response). Regulator split matters for downstream parsing: FPPC owns rules/manual/forms guidance, Secretary of State Political Reform Division owns the registry and bulk export. Statute lives at leginfo.legislature.ca.gov in Gov. Code Title 9 Ch. 6 (sections 86100-86300).
